note: Hand-curated seed corpus covering initiation-relevant topics. Merged with the scraped corpus at runtime. Add more entries here or run `npm run scrape:prabhupada` to expand.

entries.title: Why 16 rounds is the minimum
entries.text: You must chant at least sixteen rounds of Hare Krishna mantra on beads every day without fail. This is our minimum standard. If one cannot chant sixteen rounds daily, he is not fit for first initiation. Chanting is our very life — do not neglect it under any circumstances.

entries.title: The four regulative principles
entries.text: Anyone who accepts me as spiritual master must strictly follow the four principles: no meat-eating, no illicit sex, no intoxication, and no gambling. If one cannot follow, then there is no question of initiation. First train yourself for six months — then we shall consider first initiation.

entries.title: Reading books is as good as association
entries.text: My books will do the preaching. Read them regularly, and you will get the personal association of your spiritual master. If one reads Bhagavad-gītā As It Is and Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam daily and follows the instructions, that is the actual initiation.

entries.title: Attending Maṅgala Āratī
entries.text: One must rise early, before four, take bath, and attend maṅgala āratī. This is our system. Without maṅgala āratī the day is spoiled. The whole day depends on the morning program.

entries.title: Devotional service — no vacation
entries.text: Devotional service is not a part-time affair. Twenty-four hours engagement in Kṛṣṇa's service — that is our program. Some seva must be there daily, whatever your capacity — cooking, cleaning, distributing books, chanting — every action for Kṛṣṇa.

entries.title: Accepting a bona fide spiritual master
entries.text: You must accept a bona fide spiritual master, one who is coming in the disciplic succession from Kṛṣṇa. Without spiritual master no one can understand transcendental knowledge. The spiritual master opens your eyes with the torchlight of knowledge — that is initiation.

entries.title: Dīkṣā means divya-jñāna
entries.text: Dīkṣā means divya-jñāna, transcendental knowledge, and kṣapayati — vanquishes sinful reactions. When one becomes serious, he takes shelter of a spiritual master and takes initiation. From then on he is a Vaiṣṇava. The vow is to strictly follow the four regulative principles, chant sixteen rounds daily, and serve the spiritual master.

entries.title: Second initiation — Gāyatrī and brāhmaṇa thread
entries.text: After first initiation, one who is following strictly for at least six months, one year, is recommended by the temple president for second initiation. Second initiation is Gāyatrī mantra, brāhmaṇa thread — sacred thread. That means he is now qualified brāhmaṇa, can worship the Deity and cook prasādam.

entries.title: Recommendation of temple president
entries.text: One must be recommended by the temple president for initiation. The temple president knows whether the candidate is following the four principles and chanting sixteen rounds regularly. Without proper recommendation, initiation should not be given.

entries.title: Six months minimum training
entries.text: Let him first come to the temple regularly, chant sixteen rounds daily, follow the four principles strictly for six months. Then we shall consider initiation. Not that in a hurry one becomes a devotee. Steady, gradual training is required.

entries.title: SB 1.2.6 — sa vai puṁsāṁ paro dharmo
entries.text: The supreme occupation for all humanity is that by which men can attain to loving devotional service unto the transcendent Lord. Such devotional service must be unmotivated and uninterrupted to completely satisfy the self. This is the definition of first-class religion. Not that religion means some ritualistic performance. Religion means to develop love of God — pure, unmotivated, uninterrupted.

entries.title: BG 4.34 — Approach a bona fide spiritual master
entries.text: Just try to learn the truth by approaching a spiritual master. Inquire from him submissively and render service unto him. The self-realized soul can impart knowledge unto you because he has seen the truth. Three items: praṇipāta (surrender), paripraśna (submissive inquiry), sevā (service). Without these three, no one can understand the science of Kṛṣṇa.

entries.title: Detachment is not indifference
entries.text: Detachment means engaging everything in Kṛṣṇa's service, not becoming dry. Yukta-vairāgya. If you simply refuse everything, that is dry renunciation — phalgu vairāgya — and it will not stand. Real detachment is: whatever you have, engage it for Kṛṣṇa.

entries.title: Attentive chanting, not mechanical
entries.text: Chanting must be attentive. Simply moving the beads mechanically is not chanting. Hear each syllable of the mantra — that is chanting. If the mind wanders, bring it back. Practice makes it steady. Sixteen rounds attentively is far better than thirty-two rounds inattentively.
